speaker: KING
text: I prithee, Harry, withdraw thyself. Thou bleedest too much.

speaker: KING
text: Lord John of Lancaster, go you with him.

speaker: LANCASTER
text: Not I, my lord, unless I did bleed too.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I beseech your Majesty, make up,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Lest your retirement do amaze your friends.

speaker: KING
text: I will do so.—My Lord of Westmoreland,

speaker: KING
text: Lead him to his tent.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Come, my lord, I’ll lead you to your tent.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Lead me, my lord? I do not need your help,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And God forbid a shallow scratch should drive

speaker: PRINCE
text: The Prince of Wales from such a field as this,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Where stained nobility lies trodden on,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And rebels’ arms triumph in massacres.

speaker: LANCASTER
text: We breathe too long. Come, cousin Westmoreland,

speaker: LANCASTER
text: Our duty this way lies. For God’s sake, come.

speaker: PRINCE
text: By God, thou hast deceived me, Lancaster.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I did not think thee lord of such a spirit.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Before, I loved thee as a brother, John,

speaker: PRINCE
text: But now I do respect thee as my soul.

speaker: KING
text: I saw him hold Lord Percy at the point

speaker: KING
text: With lustier maintenance than I did look for

speaker: KING
text: Of such an ungrown warrior.

speaker: PRINCE
text: O, this boy lends mettle to us all.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Another king! They grow like Hydra’s heads.—

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: I am the Douglas, fatal to all those

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: That wear those colors on them. What art thou

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: That counterfeit’st the person of a king?

speaker: KING
text: The King himself, who, Douglas, grieves at heart,

speaker: KING
text: So many of his shadows thou hast met

speaker: KING
text: And not the very king. I have two boys

speaker: KING
text: Seek Percy and thyself about the field,

speaker: KING
text: But, seeing thou fall’st on me so luckily,

speaker: KING
text: I will assay thee. And defend thyself.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: I fear thou art another counterfeit,

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: And yet, in faith, thou bearest thee like a king.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: But mine I am sure thou art, whoe’er thou be,

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: And thus I win thee.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Hold up thy head, vile Scot, or thou art like

speaker: PRINCE
text: Never to hold it up again. The spirits

speaker: PRINCE
text: Of valiant Shirley, Stafford, Blunt are in my arms.

speaker: PRINCE
text: It is the Prince of Wales that threatens thee,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Who never promiseth but he means to pay.

speaker: PRINCE
text:
To
                 
                King
                .
                 
              Cheerly, my lord. How fares your Grace?

speaker: PRINCE
text: Sir Nicholas Gawsey hath for succor sent,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And so hath Clifton. I’ll to Clifton straight.

speaker: KING
text: Stay and breathe awhile.

speaker: KING
text: Thou hast redeemed thy lost opinion

speaker: KING
text: And showed thou mak’st some tender of my life

speaker: KING
text: In this fair rescue thou hast brought to me.

speaker: PRINCE
text: O God, they did me too much injury

speaker: PRINCE
text: That ever said I hearkened for your death.

speaker: PRINCE
text: If it were so, I might have let alone

speaker: PRINCE
text: The insulting hand of Douglas over you,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Which would have been as speedy in your end

speaker: PRINCE
text: As all the poisonous potions in the world,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And saved the treacherous labor of your son.

speaker: KING
text: Make up to Clifton. I’ll to Sir Nicholas Gawsey.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: If I mistake not, thou art Harry Monmouth.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Thou speak’st as if I would deny my name.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: My name is Harry Percy.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Why then I see

speaker: PRINCE
text: A very valiant rebel of the name.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I am the Prince of Wales; and think not, Percy,

speaker: PRINCE
text: To share with me in glory any more.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Two stars keep not their motion in one sphere,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Nor can one England brook a double reign

speaker: PRINCE
text: Of Harry Percy and the Prince of Wales.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Nor shall it, Harry, for the hour is come

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To end the one of us, and would to God

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Thy name in arms were now as great as mine.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I’ll make it greater ere I part from thee,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And all the budding honors on thy crest

speaker: PRINCE
text: I’ll crop to make a garland for my head.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I can no longer brook thy vanities.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Well said, Hal! To it, Hal! Nay, you shall find

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: no boys’ play here, I can tell you.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: O Harry, thou hast robbed me of my youth.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I better brook the loss of brittle life

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Than those proud titles thou hast won of me.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: They wound my thoughts worse than thy sword my flesh.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But thoughts, the slaves of life, and life, time’s fool,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And time, that takes survey of all the world,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Must have a stop. O, I could prophesy,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But that the earthy and cold hand of death

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Lies on my tongue. No, Percy, thou art dust,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And food for—

speaker: PRINCE
text: For worms, brave Percy. Fare thee well, great heart.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Ill-weaved ambition, how much art thou shrunk!

speaker: PRINCE
text: When that this body did contain a spirit,

speaker: PRINCE
text: A kingdom for it was too small a bound,

speaker: PRINCE
text: But now two paces of the vilest earth

speaker: PRINCE
text: Is room enough. This earth that bears thee dead

speaker: PRINCE
text: Bears not alive so stout a gentleman.

speaker: PRINCE
text: If thou wert sensible of courtesy,

speaker: PRINCE
text: I should not make so dear a show of zeal.

speaker: PRINCE
text: But let my favors hide thy mangled face;

speaker: PRINCE
text: And even in thy behalf I’ll thank myself

speaker: PRINCE
text: For doing these fair rites of tenderness.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Adieu, and take thy praise with thee to heaven.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Thy ignominy sleep with thee in the grave,

speaker: PRINCE
text: But not remembered in thy epitaph.

speaker: PRINCE
text: What, old acquaintance, could not all this flesh

speaker: PRINCE
text: Keep in a little life? Poor Jack, farewell.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I could have better spared a better man.

speaker: PRINCE
text: O, I should have a heavy miss of thee

speaker: PRINCE
text: If I were much in love with vanity.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Death hath not struck so fat a deer today,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Though many dearer in this bloody fray.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Emboweled will I see thee by and by;

speaker: PRINCE
text: Till then in blood by noble Percy lie.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Emboweled? If thou embowel me today, I’ll

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: give you leave to powder me and eat me too

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: tomorrow. ’Sblood, ’twas time to counterfeit, or

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: that hot termagant Scot had paid me scot and lot

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: too. Counterfeit? I lie. I am no counterfeit. To die is

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: to be a counterfeit, for he is but the counterfeit of a

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: man who hath not the life of a man; but to counterfeit

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: dying when a man thereby liveth is to be no

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: counterfeit, but the true and perfect image of life

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: indeed. The better part of valor is discretion, in the

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: which better part I have saved my life. Zounds, I am

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: afraid of this gunpowder Percy, though he be dead.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: How if he should counterfeit too, and rise? By my

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: faith, I am afraid he would prove the better counterfeit.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Therefore I’ll make him sure, yea, and I’ll swear

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I killed him. Why may not he rise as well as I?

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Nothing confutes me but eyes, and nobody sees me.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text:
Therefore, sirrah, 
                stabbing
                 
                him
               with a new wound

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: in your thigh, come you along with me.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Come, brother John. Full bravely hast thou fleshed

speaker: PRINCE
text: Thy maiden sword.

speaker: LANCASTER
text: But soft, whom have we here?

speaker: LANCASTER
text: Did you not tell me this fat man was dead?

speaker: PRINCE
text: I did; I saw him dead,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Breathless and bleeding on the ground.—Art thou alive?

speaker: PRINCE
text: Or is it fantasy that plays upon our eyesight?

speaker: PRINCE
text: I prithee, speak. We will not trust our eyes

speaker: PRINCE
text: Without our ears. Thou art not what thou seem’st.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: No, that’s certain. I am not a double man.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: But if I be not Jack Falstaff, then am I a jack. There

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: is Percy. If your father will do me any honor, so; if

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: not, let him kill the next Percy himself. I look to be

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: either earl or duke, I can assure you.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Why, Percy I killed myself, and saw thee dead.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Didst thou? Lord, Lord, how this world is

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: given to lying. I grant you, I was down and out of

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: breath, and so was he, but we rose both at an instant

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: and fought a long hour by Shrewsbury clock. If I

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: may be believed, so; if not, let them that should

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: reward valor bear the sin upon their own heads. I’ll

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: take it upon my death, I gave him this wound in

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: the thigh. If the man were alive and would deny

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: it, zounds, I would make him eat a piece of my

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: sword.

speaker: LANCASTER
text: This is the strangest tale that ever I heard.

speaker: PRINCE
text: This is the strangest fellow, brother John.—

speaker: PRINCE
text: Come bring your luggage nobly on your back.

speaker: PRINCE
text: For my part, if a lie may do thee grace,

speaker: PRINCE
text: I’ll gild it with the happiest terms I have.

speaker: PRINCE
text: The trumpet sounds retreat; the day is ours.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Come, brother, let us to the highest of the field

speaker: PRINCE
text: To see what friends are living, who are dead.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I’ll follow, as they say, for reward. He that

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: rewards me, God reward him. If I do grow great,

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I’ll grow less, for I’ll purge and leave sack and live

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: cleanly as a nobleman should do.
